title: Rewrite /retrospective to output beads + cross-repo Issues, retire retros.md
description:
The personal-infra split planning (paul-context/decisions/2026-05-03-beads-per-repo-issues-as-inbox.md) shifted the work-tracking model: beads internal to each repo, GitHub Issues as the cross-repo / sandbox-agent inbox. /retrospective still uses the old flow (appending bullets to ~/.claude/retros.md), which is reachable only on the local machine and creates a file that nothing else reads.

Target behaviour:

1. Run the same per-dimension session analysis as today (Continue / Start / Stop, plus the diagnostic dimensions: approval friction, CI round-trips, errors, pivots, prompt clarity, tool gaps, missing tools, MCP opportunities, Claude addon opportunities, memory updates, beads hygiene, slash command opportunities, etc.).

2. For each *Start* / *Stop* / actionable item, decide where the action belongs:
   - Same repo as the current cwd: bd create --title=... --type=task --priority=N here
   - Different repo (e.g. retro happened while in dotfiles but the action is about agentic-coding-config): gh issue create --repo pmgledhill102/<target-repo> ... — picked up by /start-session there via /bd-import-github-issues
   - Genuinely cross-repo or unsorted: gh issue create --repo pmgledhill102/paul-context ...

3. *Continue* items (validated patterns) and other reflective text that are not actions: skip, or capture in a bead/Issue with type=task priority=4 (low) only if the team would benefit from preserving the framing. Most Continue items are inert recall and dont need a bead.

4. Stop appending to ~/.claude/retros.md. The file is dead. Delete on next dotfiles change (dotfiles cutover PR #203 already removes it as a tracked path; retros.md sat in ~/.claude/, not in source).

Open design questions to decide while implementing:

- Should /retrospective auto-create the beads/Issues, or should it propose them and require user confirmation per item? Lean: confirm — retros are reflective and easy to over-action.
- For the "different repo" path, identifying the right target repo is non-trivial. Heuristic: the action mentions a repo name (e.g. "add brew install git-filter-repo to dotfiles") -> route there. Otherwise prompt the user.
- Sandbox-agent reachability test: a sandbox running /retrospective should still work. The sandbox has gh auth and bd in the current repos workspace; both flows work over the network.

Acceptance:
- Modified ~/.claude/commands/retrospective.md (lives in agentic-coding-config/home/commands/retrospective.md) implementing the above flow
- Per-item beads/Issues created during a real session, captured cleanly and visible at the right inbox
- ~/.claude/retros.md no longer being written to
- Documented behaviour change in the slash command itself (the doc-comment block at the top)

Related:
- paul-context/decisions/2026-05-03-beads-per-repo-issues-as-inbox.md (the design rationale)
- Bead paul-context-oed (security-process workstream: filter /bd-import-github-issues to my own issues only) — should be in place BEFORE this rewrite ships, otherwise sandbox-agent issues raised against public repos could mix with external spam.
close_reason: Shipped 2026-05-03 in commit 74b2d2a. /retrospective now routes findings to beads + Issues + memory writes per the design in paul-context/decisions/2026-05-03-beads-per-repo-issues-as-inbox.md. retros.md flow retired.

title: File upstream bd issue: pre-commit framework + bd dolt push conflict
description:
From retro: paul-context/journal/2026-05-03-personal-infra-split.md

When a repo has both .pre-commit-config.yaml AND bd is initialised (.beads/hooks/), bd dolt push fails with `fatal: this operation must be run in a work tree`. Cause: bd internally runs `git push --porcelain --force-with-lease=...` to push refs/dolt/data, which triggers the local pre-push hook. The dotfiles git-templates dispatch hook calls run_precommit pre-push, which invokes the pre-commit framework. pre-commit then runs `git diff --name-only` as part of its staged_files_only setup — and that fails because bd is operating in a non-worktree context (refs/dolt/data, etc.).

Local workaround captured as memory:
GIT_CONFIG_PARAMETERS=`'core.hooksPath=/dev/null'` PATH=/tmp/bd-modernize-shim:$PATH BEADS_HOOK_TIMEOUT=2 bd dolt push origin

Worth filing upstream because it affects:
- Anyone using bd in a repo with .pre-commit-config.yaml on macOS (without GNU coreutils, the timeout-based escape hatch in bd hooks does not fire)
- Anyone with the dotfiles-style git-templates dispatch hooks (the run_precommit dispatcher fires unconditionally if .pre-commit-config.yaml is present)

Suggested upstream fix at steveyegge/beads:
- bd dolt internal git push should set core.hooksPath=/dev/null (or pass --no-verify) for its non-worktree git operations
- Or: bd hooks should expose a sentinel env var (e.g. BD_INTERNAL_GIT=1) that hook dispatchers can check to skip

Acceptance:
- Issue filed at github.com/steveyegge/beads
- Issue references both this bead and the local memory feedback_bd_dolt_push_pre_commit_conflict.md for the workaround details
- Workaround documented in this repo (or in dotfiles) as a fallback if upstream fix is delayed
close_reason: Superseded by github.com/pmgledhill102/agentic-coding-config/issues/1. This bead was created via cd+bd-create from dotfiles cwd, which violated the /retrospective architecture rule (cross-repo findings should always be Issues, never direct beads via cd shortcut). The /retrospective slash command was tightened in 71ecf57 to forbid this explicitly. /start-session here will sweep Issue #1 back into a fresh bead via /bd-import-github-issues.

title: /end-session: detect stale ~/.claude/commands files removed from source
description:
From retro: paul-context/journal/2026-05-03-personal-infra-split.md

chezmoi does not auto-remove files from the target when they are removed from source state (unless `exact = true` is set, which has its own destructive risk for user-added files). Result: when a slash command is renamed or deleted in agentic-coding-config, the OLD file lingers at ~/.claude/commands/ on every machine that ever applied a version where it was present.

Concrete example surfaced 2026-05-03: ~/.claude/commands/bd-migrate-embedded.md is still present on the local machine even though that command was renamed to bd-modernize back in PR #142 (in dotfiles, before the personal-infra split). The file is operational dead-weight — Claude Code sees and lists it as a slash command.

Proposed enhancement to /end-session: include a check that diffs ls ~/.claude/commands/ against ls ~/.local/share/chezmoi/.../home/commands/ (or the chezmoi-resolved external path for the agentic-coding-config mount). Surface any extras as candidates for `rm`.

Acceptance:
- /end-session warns when ~/.claude/commands/ contains files not present in the source
- Optionally: same check for ~/.claude/bin/ (less common to rename, but same drift mechanism)
- The check is fast (just two `ls` + diff) so it can run unconditionally without slowing the session-end flow
- User can dismiss the warning per-session if the extras are intentional
close_reason: Superseded by github.com/pmgledhill102/agentic-coding-config/issues/2. Same correction as agentic-coding-config-orm: the cd+bd-create path was wrong, Issues are the canonical cross-repo path. /start-session will sweep Issue #2 back in.
